title: docs: replace '~' with $HOME in markdown as '~' isn't rendered properly
body:
Apparently, a tilde is currently rendered as a single space, making
a mess of command examples that use it, so use $HOME instead.

https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost-server/issues/8228

Signed-off-by: Robert P. J. Day <rpjday@crashcourse.ca>

comments.body:
Hi @rpjday. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
For some reason, I can't see the issue opening https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/docs/source/markdown/podman-build.1.md by browser.

On Tue, 28 Jan 2020, Douglas Schilling Landgraf wrote:

> For some reason, I can't see the issue opening
> https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/docs/source/markdown/podman-build.1.md
> by browser.

  it should be visible when using normal "man podman-build" in a
terminal window.

rday

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon*, *rpjday* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@rpjday Could you show us an example where this does not show up correctly?

On Tue, 28 Jan 2020, Daniel J Walsh wrote:

> @rpjday Could you show us an example where this does not show up
> correctly?

  one of the examples in an earlier email ... on my fedora 31 system,
a snippet from a terminal window running "man podman-build":

... snip ...

EXAMPLES
   Build an image using local Containerfiles
              $ podman build .

              $ podman build -f Containerfile.simple .

              $ cat  /Dockerfile | podman build -f - .
                    ^ should be there
              $ podman build -f Dockerfile.simple -f Containerfile.notsosimple .

              $ podman build -f Dockerfile.in
                                              ^ should be there
... snip ...

  is no one else seeing this?

rday

> On Tue, 28 Jan 2020, Daniel J Walsh wrote: @rpjday Could you show us an example where this does not show up correctly?
> one of the examples in an earlier email ... on my fedora 31 system, a snippet from a terminal window running "man podman-build": ... snip ... EXAMPLES Build an image using local Containerfiles $ podman build . $ podman build -f Containerfile.simple . $ cat /Dockerfile | podman build -f - . ^ should be there $ podman build -f Dockerfile.simple -f Containerfile.notsosimple . $ podman build -f Dockerfile.in ^ should be there ... snip ... is no one else seeing this? rday

$ git clone https://github.com/containers/libpod.git
$ man docs/source/markdown/podman-build.1.md

Still works for me, a second pair of eyes for double check would be nice.

On Tue, 28 Jan 2020, Daniel J Walsh wrote:

> @rpjday Could you show us an example where this does not show up
> correctly?

  in the same context (terminal window), there is a single example i
can see where $HOME is not going to help -- when a literal tilde is
part of the content. from podman-generate-kube.1.md (line 65):

      value: 1:10.3.10+maria~bionic

and how it renders in my terminal window:

      value: 1:10.3.10+maria bionic
                            ^

fortunately, i see no other examples of this. yes, i really am that
pedantic.

rday
Ok so this nroff translation for man pages is not working.

On Tue, 28 Jan 2020, Douglas Schilling Landgraf wrote:

>
>       On Tue, 28 Jan 2020, Daniel J Walsh wrote: @rpjday Could you show us an

>       one of the examples in an earlier email ... on my fedora 31 system, a snippet

>       Containerfile.simple . $ cat /Dockerfile | podman build -f - . ^ should be

> $ man docs/source/markdown/podman-build.1.md
>
> Still works for me, a second pair of eyes for double check would be
> nice.

  i had no idea that you could run "man" directly against a markdown
file ... i was just viewing the man pages that came with my podman
1.7.0 on fedora. maybe i'm misunderstanding all this.

rday

EXAMPLES Build an image using local Containerfiles $ podman build . $ podman build -f Containerfile.simple . $ cat /Dockerfile | podman build -f - . ^ should be there $ podman build -f Dockerfile.simple -f Containerfile.notsosimple . $ podman build -f Dockerfile.in ^ should be there ... snip ... is no one else seeing this? rday $ git clone https://github.com/containers/libpod.git $ man docs/source/markdown/podman-build.1.md Still works for me, a second pair of eyes for double check would be nice.

Just reproduced your issue. My bad looking to .md in man. /lgtm
